ELR Name: DELETED RECORDS
Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS.  THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE.  YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE.  DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.

Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS.  THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE.  YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE.  DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.

Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS.  THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE.  YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE.  DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.

Contaminated soils 
Project sponsors shall request landfill information for all proposed construction projects (NR/L3/CIV/066 Clause 18).
Request form can be found on Technical Authority’s Mining Team Connect page:

Contaminated soils

Request form can be found on Technical Authority’s Mining Team Connect page:

Domestic, commercial and industrial wastes tipped into sandpits. Significant quantities of landfill gas being produced. Site comprises three adjacent tips; one closed, one operational and one to commence operations. 
Landfill gas present? Yes 
Hazards associated with sites within 250m of known landfill include:
Hazardous gases
Leachate (hazardous liquids)
Contaminated soils 
Project sponsors shall request landfill information for all proposed construction projects (NR/L3/CIV/066 Clause 18).
Request form can be found on Technical Authority’s Mining Team Connect page: 
https://networkrail.sharepoint.com/sites/nrmyconnect-technicalauthority/SitePages/Mining.aspx
For further advice, contact nationalminingengineer@networkrail.co.uk
